questions.question: What does PTT stand for on a handheld radio?
questions.reason: PTT means Push To Talk and keys the transmitter while pressed.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a squelch control?

options.text: Mute noise when no valid signal is present

questions.reason: Squelch keeps the speaker quiet until a signal above threshold is received.

questions.question: What can happen if you transmit without a suitable load or antenna connected?
questions.reason: Without a proper load, reflected power can stress and damage transmitter output stages.

questions.question: What does an S-meter on a receiver usually indicate?
questions.reason: An S-meter is used to show the approximate strength of the received signal.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a repeater offset or duplex shift?

options.text: To separate transmit and receive frequencies

questions.reason: Repeaters receive on one frequency and retransmit on another, so radios use an offset between TX and RX.

questions.question: Why is using a low power setting often a good choice on a handheld radio?

options.text: It can save battery and reduce unnecessary interference
It removes the need for a callsign

questions.reason: Low power can be sufficient for local contacts while conserving battery and limiting unnecessary RF output.

questions.question: What is receiver selectivity?

options.text: Ability to separate wanted and nearby unwanted signals
How fast the radio scans memory channels
Output power into a dummy load

questions.reason: Selectivity describes how well a receiver rejects adjacent-channel interference.

questions.question: What can happen if FM microphone gain is set too high?
questions.reason: Excessive audio drive can cause over-deviation and poorer signal quality.

questions.question: What is the purpose of CTCSS or tone squelch on many FM systems?

options.text: To control when the receiver audio unmutes

questions.reason: Tone squelch is used to reduce nuisance audio by requiring a matching tone to open squelch.

questions.question: What is a transceiver?

options.text: A separate power supply and battery pack
A combined transmitter and receiver in one unit

questions.reason: A transceiver integrates transmit and receive functions in one radio.

questions.question: Why might an attenuator be switched in on a receiver?

options.text: To help when very strong signals overload the front end

questions.reason: Attenuation can improve reception in very strong-signal environments by reducing overload.

questions.question: If a handheld cannot key a repeater, what is a sensible first check?

options.text: Whether frequency shift and access tone are set correctly
Whether the speaker volume is maximum
Whether the battery label is new

questions.reason: Incorrect repeater offset or access tone is a common reason for failed repeater access.
